summary:
• Strait of Hormuz disruptions and geopolitical tensions with Iran pose acute supply route risks for global oil flows, with Trump administration engagement efforts and war-powers deadlines creating uncertainty around resolution timelines.

• Mexican production faces structural headwinds from Pemex's operational challenges including a major oil spill, while Canadian supply expansion through new pipeline approvals offers partial Western Hemisphere offset but faces renewable energy policy resistance.

• Russia and China are capturing market share through alternative supply arrangements as Asia experiences fuel shortages, with OPEC+ output increases and global energy system stress potentially creating volatile price swings that affect investment decisions across the Americas.
